purpose: proves dry-run stays separate from apply and inspect-first recovery still depends on fresh live hashes

pull_handoff.exporter: scans the merge base and coverage evidence
pull_handoff.importer: persists the base package as immutable provenance
pull_handoff.preflight: binds the persisted pull base to the live remote identity and a short-lived push session
pull_handoff.snapshot_hashes: lists live remote hashes for planning only and never acts as write authority
pull_handoff.dry_run: uploads the canonical plan as eligibility evidence and returns a receipt, not a lock
pull_handoff.apply: revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and again at the storage boundary
pull_handoff.journal: reads durable evidence without authorizing mutation
pull_handoff.recovery_inspect: starts with inspect and classifies finish, rollback, retry, or block before any mutating repair

apply_revalidation.at_storage_boundary: fresh live hashes plus storage-guard proof

required_invariants: dry-run and apply are separate remote operations
dry-run is a receipt, not a lock
the remote may drift between dry-run and apply
apply must revalidate the live remote before every batch and at the storage boundary
inspect is read-only and must happen before any mutating recovery
auth must be at least as strict as current Reprint HMAC usage
